title: Any Medical Instrument Can Be Considered A Sharp
source_filename: Any Medical Instrument Can Be Considered A Sharp.docx

qas.a: What should be used for all patient encounters?
We should consider all sharp items contaminated with patient blood and saliva to be what?
What 2 things should we use to prevent injuries?
What is the name for procedures that reduce the likelihood of exposure?
What is the name for tangible items that isolate or remove the BBP hazard from the work place?
never recap a needle with _____ hands, whether that be the operator's or the assistant's
What are 3 safe methods for recapping needles?
Where should sharps containers be relative to where they were used?
When should non-reusable sharps be disposed of?

qas.q: False, it's never okay to bend a needle
qas.a: T/F: Sometimes it's okay to bend needles as long as you use a mechanical device rather than your hands.

qas.q: mechanical retraction (of obstructing structures i.e. the cheek)
qas.a: What is recommended when administering an injection?

turn handpieces and power scaler tips away from the _________, toward the _________
or
cover with plastic sheath or protective cover to avoid puncture and scratch injuries
use caution while handling _________ instruments
T/F: You should never wipe debris from instrument tips with gauze held in your hand or wrapped around fingers

qas.q: False, you should never finger rest on the same tooth on which you are working
qas.a: T/F: during scaling and cutting, you should rest your finger on the same tooth on which you are working
three types of engineering controls that could modify devices to increase safety measures in practice:

qas.q:
-Closable
-Hard-walled, puncture proof, leak-proof (sides and bottom), spill proof sharps container.
-Labeled appropriately
-Remain upright throughout use
-Must be located in operatories and other locations where disposable sharps are generated and used.
qas.a: requirements for sharps containers:
When should sharps containers be replaced?

qas.q: FALSE; sharps containers should not be reused
qas.a: T/F: One should empty a sharps container before reusing it.

qas.q:
1. close the container
2. dispose of as regulated waste
qas.a: steps for removing a container of contaminated sharps:
who created the Needlestick Safety and Prevention Act?
Which part of the OSHA Bloodborne Pathogens Standard is mandated?
safety needles, safety syringes, safety scalpels and sutures, needleless IV catheters, ports and injection systems are all:

qas.q:
-annually
-as they become available in the market
qas.a: When should dental employers assess safer sharps devices?
If inquiries indicate safer options for sharps devices are not available, where should this be documented?
sharps assessment process must involve which employees that are involved in direct patient care (identifying and choosing devices)?

qas.q:
1. screening devices (bench test)
2. evaluating devices (pilot clinical test)
qas.a: Steps of the sharps assessment process:

qas.q:
-Intuitive
-Efficient change of carpules
-Visibility of needle tip and carpule contents
-Adaptable to various hand sizes
-Easy, safe breakdown and disposal
-Cost effective
qas.a: desirable characteristics of a dental syringe/needle

qas.q: before bringing them into patient-case setting
qas.a: When should you make decisions concerning clinical and safety considerations of sharps?

qas.q:
1. review MFG instructions
2. manipulate the device to become familiar
3. test using oranges or other substitute
4. involve employees from all categories that will handle the device
5. determine proper disposal methods and practices
6. use or modify the CDC screening form
qas.a: What are the steps of the Bench test screening?
A failure for a sharps safety device results in what?

qas.q: staff receives training on correct use of the device
qas.a: What is the first step of the evaluation phase of the pilot test?

qas.q:
-staff uses device
-documents experience by feedback
-completes eval form
qas.a: What is the second step of the evaluation phase of the pilot test?

qas.q:
-employer, ICC or safety officer monitors if device is used properly
-stops pilot test if the device is found to be unsafe
qas.a: What is the third step of the evaluation phase of the pilot test?

qas.q: at the end of pilot test, staff discusses and completes an eval form
qas.a: What is the fourth step of the evaluation phase of the pilot test?

qas.q:
-findings are reviewed by employer, ICC or safety officer
-decision is made about continuing the use of new device in practice
qas.a: What is the fifth (final) step of the evaluation phase of the pilot test?
(simplecap) What is the clear tube that protects the needle?
(simplecap) What is the colored system that holds the needle?
(simplecap) What is the colored packaging that SimpleCAP comes in?
(simplecap) What opens gaps in the sheath where pins sit between injections?
(simplecap) What are the slots that run the length of the sheath?
(simplecap) What are the thin plastic tabs that ride inside of the channels and locks?
What is a percutaneous injury or contact of mucous membrane or non-intact skin with blood, tissue or other body fluids that are potentially infectious?
What mandates making hep B vaccine available to all employees who might have occupational exposure as well as the management of post-exposures to BBP according to US public health services?
Whose responsibility is it to make sure that a confidential medical evaluation and follow-up are immediately available to an employee after report of an exposure incident?
source individual's ________ to testing of blood to determine HBV and HIV infectivity; if this is not give, this should be documented
when should source individual's blood be tested?
results of the source individual's testing shall be made available to the ______________ _________
What cannot be identified by an exposed employee after they receive the results of their testing?

qas.q:
-Whether hep B vaccination is indicated and if received by the employee
-Employee has been informed of the results of the evaluation
qas.a: Written opinion from HCP to employer only include:

qas.q:
-Results of the source individual's blood testing, if available
(Usually exchanged between physicians)
-All medical records relevant to employee treatment
(Vaccination status)
qas.a: employer must provide the evaluating HCP:
What should we consider a needle stick injury?

qas.q:
1. Immediately STOP activity

2. Wash the injured area with soap and water

3. Flush mucous membranes with copious
amounts of water, if involved

4. Render FIRST AID if needed
qas.a: Initial steps for post-exposure protocol:

qas.q:
-report the incident
-complete the incident report
-medical eval
qas.a: What steps should be taken post-exposure?

qas.q:
Team coordinator and
Ed Rangel or Claudia Nunes
qas.a: Who should an incident be reported to?
What are the components of a post-exposure evaluation?
